changes.detail: All five mandatory front matter fields present and correct: work_id (CF-AUTHORITY-RUNTIME-1_runtime_signal_authority_collision_enforcement), branch (work/CF-AUTHORITY-RUNTIME-1-runtime-signal-authority-collision-enforcement), risk_level (HIGH), execution_model (TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH), change_type (MIXED). No field missing.

HIGH is correct and mandatory. Sprint modifies SignalEvaluator.evaluate_all() — a direct Intelligence Core component. Any work touching Intelligence Core = HIGH regardless of file path (SOP §10). MIXED is also correct: governance YAML updates (CONTENT) + evaluator/result assembly runtime changes (BEHAVIOUR) are both in scope. MIXED is governed by BEHAVIOUR controls per SOP §4. No reclassification warranted.

TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH is correct. Phase 1 = design preflight report (no code). Phase 2 = runtime implementation + tests + governance register + report. A mandatory human STOP gate (READY_FOR_HUMAN_STOP_GATE + APPROVE BATCH2 EGFR AUTHORITY ACTIVATION) separates Phase 2 completion from Phase 3 eGFR activation. This is structurally sound and appropriate for HIGH/Intelligence Core work.

File exists. schema_version: 1.0.0. runtime_consumed: false (line 2). renal_filtration_axis authority group defined at lines 11-58: primary_signal_family=signal_egfr_low, supporting_signal_families=[signal_creatinine_high], collision_policy.no_duplicate_user_facing_signal=true, collision_policy.suppress_supporting_when_primary_present=true, collision_policy.allow_parallel_if_distinct_risk_layer=true, runtime_action=governance_only_pending_runtime_support, requires_runtime_support=true. proposed_runtime_pattern documented at lines 46-54. Six placeholder authority groups for future axes confirmed (metabolic_glycaemic, thyroid, androgen, liver_injury, iron_inflammation). No duplicate authority source.

File exists. status: authority_adjudicated_pending_runtime_support (line 3). runtime_consumed: false (line 2). human_stop_gate.required: true, approval_received: false (lines 8-14). anti_double_counting_decision.mechanism_exists_in_evaluator: false (line 29). activation_blocked_until: CF-AUTHORITY-RUNTIME-1 (line 33). Two packages documented at lines 37-68: pkg_kb47_egfr_low_chronic_kidney_function_reduction (activation_key: signal_egfr_low::inv_egfr_low_chronic_kidney_function_reduction) and pkg_kb47_egfr_low_hemodynamic_filtration_drop (activation_key: signal_egfr_low::inv_egfr_low_hemodynamic_filtration_drop). Both activated: false, gate_implemented: false.

Package exists. signal_id: signal_egfr_low. activation_logic: lab_range_exceeded. DEFECT: activation_config.enable_lower_bound: false (line 51). For a low-direction signal, enable_lower_bound must be true and enable_upper_bound must be false (pattern confirmed in working creatinine_low package lines 57-59: enable_upper_bound: false, enable_lower_bound: true). Current state means both eGFR signals will NEVER fire — they are de-facto inactive via broken activation_config. Phase 3 activation MUST fix both packages to enable_lower_bound: true, enable_upper_bound: false. This is a known required change, not a scope blocker.

Package exists. signal_id: signal_egfr_low. activation_logic: lab_range_exceeded. DEFECT: same activation_config.enable_lower_bound: false as chronic package. Phase 3 must also fix this package. Activation keys are distinct from chronic package (source_spec_id inferred as inv_egfr_low_hemodynamic_filtration_drop from package_id). No duplicate activation_key collision on load. Both eGFR packages co-exist in SignalRegistry without error but produce zero signals because enable_lower_bound is false.

Two active creatinine-high packages in runtime. pkg_s24_creatinine_high_renal: signal_creatinine_high, activation_logic=lab_range_exceeded (upper bound active by default). COLLISION RISK: override rule or_renal_ckd_stage_3 (egfr < 60, line 48) is the exact filtration-duplicate to suppress when eGFR-low is primary. Override rule or_renal_acute_imbalance (potassium > 5.2, line 54) is the DISTINCT RISK LAYER that must remain allowed per collision_policy.allow_parallel_if_distinct_risk_layer=true. pkg_kb52c_creatinine_high_reduced_glomerular_filtration: signal_creatinine_high, enable_upper_bound: true, UACR-based override — also a filtration duplicate to suppress when eGFR primary.

File exists. signal_egfr_low family at lines 1033-1093. frame_egfr_pass3_chronic_kidney_function_reduction (lines 1037-1065): promotion_state=compiled_not_promoted, runtime_authority_status=inactive, collision_status=allowed_non_runtime_collision, notes confirm CF-AUTHORITY-RUNTIME-1 as required next action (lines 1063-1065). frame_egfr_pass3_hemodynamic_filtration_drop (lines 1066-1093): same status, ROUTE_C multi-frame preserved confirmed (line 1090). No runtime-active eGFR frames. Creatinine family: frame_creatinine_legacy_s24_egfr_escalation (lines 41-68) is runtime_active_legacy_unadjudicated (the escalation-path duplicate that must be suppressed at the signal level, not the package level).

File exists. evaluate_all() at lines 440-534: loads all active signals from registry, evaluates each independently, returns all that fire. NO cross-signal suppression. NO authority group lookup. NO family-level collision detection. Only inter-signal mechanism is duplicate activation_key ValueError at load time (lines 59-64 in SignalRegistry._load). The correct enforcement point for authority/collision is POST-evaluate_all() filtering — inject authority resolution between evaluate_all() result assembly and the caller. This is the minimum-invasive, reusable pattern.

File exists. activation_key = signal_id::source_spec_id. For eGFR packages: source_spec_id inferred from package_id via _PKG_BODY_RE pattern (line 48): pkg_kb47_egfr_low_chronic_kidney_function_reduction -> inv_egfr_low_chronic_kidney_function_reduction. pkg_kb47_egfr_low_hemodynamic_filtration_drop -> inv_egfr_low_hemodynamic_filtration_drop. Both produce distinct activation_keys — no collision on load. Collision enforcement must operate on signal_id or signal_family grouping, not activation_key.

All scripts required by prompt exist: run_architecture_validation_gate.py, validate_medical_frame_identity_index.py, validate_context_modifier_catalogue.py, validate_knowledge_package.py. No missing tooling.

File exists. CF-AUTHORITY-RUNTIME-1 at line 78: Status=Open, source=BATCH2-EGFR-AUTHORITY-1, prerequisite before eGFR metadata activation confirmed. CF-BATCH2-007 at line 70: Status=Resolved — IMPORTANT: Resolved means the adjudication-task carry-forward was completed by BATCH2-EGFR-AUTHORITY-1; both eGFR packages remain compiled_not_promoted_inactive. CF-CONTEXT-MOD-3 at line 77: Status=Open, androgen/FT3 blocker — unrelated to this sprint. Do NOT reopen CF-BATCH2-007.

signal_authority_collision_model_v1.yaml is the single renal filtration authority source. creatinine_multiframe_authority_decision_v1.yaml is the creatinine frame authority source — distinct scope. medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml is the frame identity registry — not an authority model. No new SSOT files required. The new execution register (authority_runtime_execution_register_v1.yaml) is a sprint-specific record, not an authority source. No duplicate authority source risk.

Intelligence Core surface area: SignalEvaluator.evaluate_all() return-value post-processing is the single allowed enforcement point. Correct pattern: evaluate_all() returns full results → authority/collision resolver reads signal_authority_collision_model_v1.yaml → suppresses supporting-family signals when primary-family is present → returns filtered results. Alternative: inject as a method on SignalEvaluator or as a separate authority resolver class called by the pipeline. Either is acceptable. The resolver must NOT modify the underlying signal evaluation logic — only filter the assembled result list.

8 required regression tests explicitly listed. Key behaviours: (1) eGFR-low emits when active and criteria met, (2) creatinine-high filtration duplicate suppressed when eGFR-low present, (3) creatinine remains as supporting evidence where appropriate, (4) distinct acute risk layers (potassium/electrolyte) not suppressed, (5) unrelated signals unaffected, (6) authority model fail-safe when missing/malformed, (7) eGFR inactive before STOP approval, (8) only two eGFR packages activate after approval. All 8 are testable against verified source behaviour.

Four determinism risks Cursor must handle: (1) Authority model loader must fail-closed — if YAML missing or malformed, evaluator must not silently pass all signals; must raise or return empty/error. (2) Filtered output must maintain sort order (signal_id, activation_key) matching current evaluate_all sort at lines 532-534. (3) eGFR activation_config fix (enable_lower_bound: true, enable_upper_bound: false) applies to BOTH packages — missing one causes asymmetric behaviour. (4) Suppression must target signal_creatinine_high signals from both pkg_s24 (or_renal_ckd_stage_3 override) and pkg_kb52c — not the potassium distinct-risk-layer. No silent output drift allowed.

changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 235-243 (do not change list), lines 311 (do not activate androgen), lines 312-314 (creatinine constraint)
changes.detail: Explicit do-not-change list in prompt: clinical wording, thresholds, reference ranges, signal IDs, activation keys, frontend, SSOT, scoring thresholds, unit conversion, report compiler. Androgen packages explicitly excluded. Creatinine package logic must not change unless strictly required and explicitly justified. The activation_config fix in eGFR signal_library.yaml files is NOT in the do-not-change list and is the defined activation mechanism — acceptable as Phase 3 change post STOP approval.

changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 74-84 (baseline STOP), lines 205-214 (Phase 1 STOP), lines 489-503 (closure STOP conditions)
changes.detail: Baseline STOP covers: wrong branch, main drift, dirty tree, missing authority model, missing eGFR packages, missing creatinine frames, gate failure. Phase 1 STOP covers: authority model not safely loadable, no safe enforcement point, requires broad redesign, complication layers would be suppressed, rollback undefined. Closure STOP covers: wrong branch, out-of-scope files, androgen files changed, frontend/SSOT/scoring changed, stash present, validators fail, gate fails. Coverage is comprehensive. No STOP condition gap found.

Rollback path is definable: revert signal_evaluator.py authority resolver addition, revert eGFR signal_library.yaml activation_config changes, revert authority_runtime_execution_register_v1.yaml, revert medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml eGFR frame status updates (if any), revert carry-forward register updates. All changes are file-local and reversible. No DB migration, no schema change, no irreversible operation. Rollback path confirmed viable.

changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 64-73 (baseline commands), lines 444-461 (validation commands), lines 512-519 (closure commands)
changes.detail: All shell commands use python backend/scripts/... and git invocations. No bash-specific syntax detected. Validation scripts are Python — compatible with Windows PowerShell. git commands follow standard form.

MANDATORY: Both eGFR signal_library.yaml files have activation_config.enable_lower_bound: false. This is a defect — for a low-direction signal using lab_range_exceeded, enable_lower_bound must be true and enable_upper_bound must be false (pattern confirmed in pkg_kb52c_creatinine_low_low_muscle_mass_or_low_generation). Phase 3 activation MUST change both packages to: enable_lower_bound: true, enable_upper_bound: false. Without this fix, eGFR signals will never fire and activation will appear to succeed but produce no clinical output. This change to signal_library.yaml is specifically required for activation and is within Phase 3 scope.

MANDATORY: Authority/collision enforcement must occur as a post-processing step on the results list returned by evaluate_all(). Do NOT modify the individual signal evaluation logic (threshold, override, gate methods). The pattern: evaluate_all() returns List[SignalResult] → authority resolver reads renal_filtration_axis from signal_authority_collision_model_v1.yaml → groups results by signal family (signal_egfr_low, signal_creatinine_high) → if primary family (signal_egfr_low) has any active results, filter supporting-family (signal_creatinine_high) results that are renal-filtration duplicates → preserve signal_creatinine_high results whose override logic fired for distinct risk layers (potassium). The authority model's proposed_runtime_pattern.authority_resolution block (lines 46-54 of collision model) defines this exactly.

MANDATORY: The potassium/electrolyte complication layer (signal_creatinine_high override or_renal_acute_imbalance, potassium > 5.2) is a DISTINCT RISK LAYER per the authority model (lines 27-31 of collision model: hyperkalemia_or_electrolyte_complication must remain parallel when medically distinct). The suppression logic must NOT suppress signal_creatinine_high results that are driven by the potassium override path. Only suppress creatinine-high results that represent filtration-level duplicates (or_renal_ckd_stage_3 override or general creatinine-high without distinct-risk escalation). Failure to preserve this layer is a clinical safety violation.

CF-BATCH2-007 shows Status: Resolved. Cursor must NOT interpret this as eGFR being activated or authority-enforced. Resolved means the adjudication carry-forward task was completed by BATCH2-EGFR-AUTHORITY-1 which recorded the blocker in signal_authority_collision_model_v1.yaml. Both eGFR packages remain compiled_not_promoted with runtime_authority_status: inactive (medical_frame_identity_index_v1.yaml lines 1047-1048 and 1073-1074). This sprint (CF-AUTHORITY-RUNTIME-1) is the one that resolves the actual blocking condition.

prior_task_findings.BATCH2-EGFR-AUTHORITY-1: CONFIRMED: signal_authority_collision_model_v1.yaml created with runtime_consumed=false. renal_filtration_axis authority group adjudicated. Both eGFR packages authority_adjudicated_pending_runtime_support. phase_3_outcome=B (governance only). No runtime code changed. anti_double_counting_decision.mechanism_exists_in_evaluator=false confirmed. Source: batch2_egfr_authority_execution_register_v1.yaml lines 1-68 (entire file read).
prior_task_findings.BATCH2-REMAINING-BLOCKERS-1: CONFIRMED: status=analysis_complete_ready_for_human_stop_gate. runtime_code_changed=false. activated_package_count=0. eGFR blocker confirmed as renal_authority_and_anti_double_counting_required. androgen (8 packages) and FT3-low still blocked by CF-CONTEXT-MOD-3 — unrelated to this sprint. Source: batch2_remaining_blockers_execution_register_v1.yaml lines 1-30 read.

hardening_notes: Sprint is hardened with two mandatory implementation instructions: (1) Both eGFR signal_library.yaml files have enable_lower_bound: false — this must be fixed to enable_lower_bound: true in Phase 3 activation. Without this fix, eGFR signals will never fire. (2) Collision enforcement must target only renal-filtration duplicate outputs from signal_creatinine_high, NOT the distinct potassium/electrolyte risk layer. All authority paths verified against source file content with line citations. SignalEvaluator.evaluate_all() confirmed to have no existing collision mechanism. Enforcement insertion point confirmed as post-evaluate_all() filtering. No blocking SOP violations found. No STOP condition triggered. Sprint may proceed to Stage 3 (python backend/scripts/run_work_package.py start).
